items.title: Oil falls as easing Middle East tensions send Brent to its worst quarter since 2020
items.description: Oil prices fell more than 1% on Wednesday after talks between Washington and Tehran concluded.

items.title: Samsung Electronics, SK Hynix shares tumble over 7% as chip rout spreads from Wall Street
items.description: Shares of Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix plummeted more than 7% in early Thursday trading.

items.title: Alibaba-affiliate Ant Group rushes into humanoid robots with a dozen deals in 18 months
items.description: Ant has led a 500 million yuan ($73.59 million) funding round in humanoid robotics company Zeroth, the start-up announced Thursday.

items.title:
Inside India newsletter: Amazon, Walmart-owned Flipkart get ready to shake up
India's delivery-in-minutes sector
items.description: Amazon and Walmart-owned Flipkart are scrambling to expand quick commerce operations in India, not just to chase growth but to remain relevant to consumers.

items.title: South Korean government discriminated against Coupang, U.S. companies, House report finds
items.description: The House Judiciary Committee said the South Korean government discriminated against Coupang and other U.S. companies, in a new report.

items.title: Why Japanese airlines' $400 fuel surcharges are hitting now — and when they will end
items.description: Fuel surcharges at Japan Airlines and All Nippon Airways increased Wednesday, despite falling jet fuel costs in Asia.

items.title: U.S. auto industry faces increased uncertainty without extension of USMCA trade deal
items.description: A major issue for automakers is the deal's rules of origin, which determine where a product comes from and which goods are eligible for preferential treatment.

items.title: Jim Cramer says to use Wednesday's market rotation to your advantage. Here's how he'd play it
items.description: CNBC’s Jim Cramer said investors should use the latest market rotation to buy some of the biggest winners they may have missed.

items.title: Trump got $15,000 in FIFA tickets from Gianni Infantino. Next up: Presenting the World Cup trophy
items.description: Trump’s ties to FIFA and Gianni Infantino face new scrutiny as the World Cup enters the knockout round and European lawmakers press over a Peace Prize complaint.

items.title: Prolific tech analyst Dan Ives is exiting Wedbush for a new venture
items.description: Ives said the new firm will seek to build what he described as a "modern merchant bank."

items.title: World Cup could boost the June jobs report by 40,000, Goldman estimates
items.description: Nonfarm payrolls are projected to post a gain of 115,000, according to the Dow Jones consensus.

items.title: Automakers report mixed U.S. sales results as hybrid vehicles drive market
items.description: Second-quarter U.S. new vehicle sales show a sharp divide, with automakers offering hybrid models largely outperforming those without  them.

items.title: Chip stocks that notched record rallies in second quarter start Q3 with a dud
items.description: Memory maker Micron, which jumped over 240% in the second quarter, dropped 11% on Wednesday, wiping out nearly $200 billion of market capitalization.

items.title: PlayStation will end physical disc production for new games in 2028
items.description: PlayStation will end physical disc production for new games in 2028, the company announced Wednesday morning.

items.title: Meta pops 9% as company makes cloud push to sell excess AI compute power capacity
items.description: The new business is a welcome signal for some investors who have been uneasy about the company's infrastructure spending plans.

items.title: Palantir's Karp bashes OpenAI, Anthropic token model: 'Something has gone completely wrong'
items.description: Karp says skyrocketing token costs are forcing companies to choose open-weight models and prioritize efficiency over so-called tokenmaxxing

items.title: Meta's plan to launch a cloud business eases the biggest overhang on the stock
items.description: The stock's struggles in 2026 had been due to concerns about its aggressive AI spending.

items.title: Trump wants delay but E. Jean Carroll wants to gets paid — now: Court filing
items.description: The Supreme Court on Monday denied a request by President Donald Trump that it review a jury's verdict that he sexually abused and defamed the writer.

items.title: U.S. won’t renew USMCA, opening door for negotiations with Canada and Mexico
items.description: President Donald Trump's "primary" issue with USMCA centers on America's trade deficits with Canada and Mexico, a senior administration official said.

items.title: Trump says outside funds 'run my money' after disclosure shows billions in 2025 revenue
items.description: The 927-page annual financial disclosure form shows Trump's purchases and sales of hundreds of companies' stocks, including Nvidia, Amazon and Microsoft.
